dialogue.headline:
The script of our era has been torn apart, and the once-unified melody has scattered into fragments.
In this age of chaos, can we find our balance again?
dialogue.paragraph1: As we stand in 2026, we—as students and people in tech—find ourselves at the crest of an era in flux. The rise of AI is challenging long-standing ways of building software, and the technical barriers that once defined our value are steadily falling away. When the power to create becomes democratized and individual technical skill can no longer be our moat, collaboration becomes our central challenge. We are moving toward a magnificent chaos: a search for balance as we co-create with AI, our peers, and partners across disciplines. But what if chaos is not an obstacle, but the beginning of a new equilibrium?
dialogue.paragraph2: In an improvised Jam Session, there is no conductor. When the beat falls out of sync, we create a new rhythm together. When the harmony is imperfect, it becomes the voice of our generation—inclusive, bold, unrestrained, and alive with many voices. At SITCON 2026, we invite you to join this Jam. Let us navigate chaos, find rhythm amid disorder, and seek resonance through collaboration.
dialogue.paragraph3: Founded in 2012, the Students’ Information Technology Conference (SITCON) is a student-centered conference initiated and organized by students. SITCON has long been dedicated to student IT education and the promotion of open source. It hopes to guide more students into the world of information technology and bring together students interested in tech to exchange ideas, pass knowledge forward, and inspire one another—putting into practice the spirit of learning by doing and teaching through sharing.

dialog.fullParagraph:
As we stand in 2026, we—as students and people in tech—find ourselves at the crest of an era in flux. The rise of AI is challenging long-standing ways of building software, and the technical barriers that once defined our value are steadily falling away. When the power to create becomes democratized and individual technical skill can no longer be our moat, collaboration becomes our central challenge. We are moving toward a magnificent chaos: a search for balance as we co-create with AI, our peers, and partners across disciplines.

But what if chaos is not an obstacle, but the beginning of a new equilibrium? In an improvised Jam Session, there is no conductor. The timbre, beat, and character of the music emerge entirely from the performers’ interaction and shared intuition. That means the key is no longer to play the most beautiful solo; it is to become the kind of musician who knows how to listen, interpret, anticipate, and respond to every collaborator’s needs, creating value through interaction with partners of all kinds. Yet when we share the stage with AI, our peers, and cross-disciplinary partners—each a different musician in the same Jam—this performance will bring its own variations and challenges.

When we jam with AI, we must actively define the boundaries of collaboration. Over-reliance on AI can cost us deep thinking, while rejecting AI outright may cause us to miss a revolution in efficiency. As perhaps the last generation to learn programming “purely by hand,” we are searching for ways to keep the developer’s soul alive.

When we jam with fellow technologists, we must redefine what value means. The focus shifts from the depth of individual technical skill to the breadth of integrating collective wisdom. While continuing to sharpen our own abilities, we must also learn how to guide and listen, forging stronger consensus amid divergence.

When we jam with cross-disciplinary partners, we must take the initiative to become translators in a cross-boundary symphony. As information technology is no longer our exclusive domain, we translate our technical language into melodies that can resonate with mathematicians, physicists, designers, and artists, creating entirely new value together.

When the beat of the Jam falls out of sync, let us create a new rhythm together.
When the harmony of the Jam is imperfect, it becomes the voice of our generation—inclusive, bold, unrestrained, and alive with many voices.

At SITCON 2026, we invite you to join this Jam. Let us navigate chaos, find rhythm amid disorder, and seek resonance through collaboration.
dialog.title: We Invite You to Join Us

featured.name: The Value of Being On-Site: How Do We Approach Truth in the AI Era?
Taiwan’s Unprecedented Open Source Brawl: How Code Can Shake the World
Is Your Brain Being Hosted? When Homework and Thinking Are Outsourced to AI

demoShow.subtitle: Brand New in 2026
demoShow.title: Demo Showcase
demoShow.description: Submissions to the Demo Showcase center on live project demonstrations—such as web games, interactive apps, robots, and other works that attendees can try or operate on-site—supported by static posters and short videos to present individual or team projects.

coc.paragraph2:

and help create a friendly and inclusive environment.

referral.title: Invite Friends & Win Rewards
referral.description1: Join SITCON 2026 in spreading the open-source spirit to more people.
referral.description2: Share your exclusive referral code with friends! When they successfully register, you’ll accumulate referrals, enter the prize draw, and have a chance to become the “Top Referrer.”
referral.description3: The more you refer, the more chances you have to win. Invite friends and earn yourself an extra souvenir.

joinUs.description:
Since 2013, SITCON has upheld its founding principle of free admission,
dedicating itself to student IT education. We encourage participants to break free from conventional thinking, explore hidden possibilities,
and gain valuable opportunities to learn and connect. Over the years, we have accompanied countless students into the world of information technology.
If you share SITCON’s vision, we welcome your support in helping the seeds of IT education spread to every corner of Taiwan.

teamPage.title: Team Members
teamPage.description:
SITCON is made possible every year by volunteers who dedicate their time and energy. Without their contributions, this rich and high-quality conference would not exist.
If you are interested in joining the organizing team, feel free to fill out the interest form or follow the SITCON mailing list. You’ll receive updates when next year’s preparation begins!

car.title: Car
car.description: Parking spaces are available within Academia Sinica. Visitors can exchange ID for a pass at the main gate security. For parking fees, please refer to Academia Sinica Car Parking Regulations.

motorcycle.title: Motorcycle
motorcycle.description: Motorcycles are prohibited from entering the campus. Free parking (no custody provided) is available at the right side of the main gate, across from the main gate, and near Hu-Shih Park.

activities.title: Keynote
activities.body: This session takes you straight to the heart of this year’s conference theme. We have invited a speaker to break down the core ideas in a clear and accessible way. Through the speaker’s perspective, you will not only understand what SITCON hopes to convey this year, but also follow the rhythm of the talk to brainstorm fresh ideas and uncover deeper insights together.

activities.title: Panel
activities.body: This is a deep and engaging conversation featuring one moderator and three panelists. Guided by the moderator’s thoughtful facilitation, guests from diverse backgrounds will explore this year’s theme from different angles. You will hear multiple perspectives collide on stage, helping you step outside the box, re-examine the core issues from new standpoints, and piece together a broader and richer technical vision through shared experience.

activities.title: Sessions
activities.body: Here you will find excellent talks submitted by students from many backgrounds. Whether you prefer in-depth Presentations that thoroughly unpack technical concepts, or fast-paced Espresso sessions that deliver key takeaways in a short time, there is plenty to discover. On top of that, there are flexible open-format sessions where ideas from different backgrounds can freely interweave. Come experience the practical knowledge and boundless creativity brought by student speakers on-site!

activities.title: Demo Showcase
activities.body:
Building on last year’s enthusiastic response, the Demo Showcase returns with an exciting upgrade! This special exhibition area goes beyond the regular program, providing a stage for students to present their projects and research through live demos and conversations. It is a great opportunity for attendees to explore diverse topics in a relaxed way and spark new exchanges and inspiration.

The showcase will take place on March 28, 2026, the day of the conference, at the booths on the north side of the 4th floor, featuring outstanding student work from across Taiwan. Whether you are interested in technical innovation, project sharing, interactive design, or cutting-edge research discoveries, you are sure to find topics that surprise and inspire you.

In addition, project exhibitors will be present at different time slots to chat and discuss with attendees. This is a perfect chance to ask questions, learn, and meet amazing peers from around the country. If you love exploring new knowledge, don’t miss it!

sections.title: First Time Here & Want to Make Friends

activities.title: Compass Project
activities.body:
Looking forward to meeting like-minded peers at the conference? We sincerely invite you to join the Compass Project!

The Compass Project provides an opportunity for exchange, helping you find partners with shared interests to explore topics together. Whether you are an experienced participant or a curious newcomer, you can find resonance here.

Based on the information you provide, we will match you with suitable partners and form groups. We also encourage everyone to actively interact with their matched partners. To ensure a good experience for all, please stay in touch with your group members after signing up and participate actively, so that every participant looking forward to interaction can truly meet others. During the conference, you can join various activities with your group members or have a relaxed meetup during lunch. Whether you share experiences, exchange viewpoints, or discuss conference sessions in depth, it will be a rewarding experience.

No matter whether this is your first time here or you already know the IT field well, everyone can find their place here and work together to create an open environment for mutual exchange and learning. We look forward to your participation and to making SITCON 2026 even more exciting together!

activities.title: Guided Tours
activities.body:
A scene of tangled chaos lies before you. In a melody of interwoven crowds, perhaps you still feel a little lost; the laughter throughout the venue is like scattered notes with no fixed beat. What are you waiting for? Join a Guided Tour! Let our conductors guide you through the unknowns of SITCON and help you find your own melody and sense of belonging. At the tour meeting point, you will find guides holding SITCON flags. Follow the SITCON guides as they lead you to community booths and the Demo Showcase, helping you understand the many facets of SITCON and how the conference works. There will be two tours in the morning and one in the afternoon. Details are as follows:
TimeMeeting Point
11:10 AM3F Whiteboard Area near R1 (Please arrive 10 minutes early)
11:20 AM
2:45 PM
Join a Guided Tour and unveil the excitement of the conference with new friends!

sections.title: Want to Play Something at the Venue

activities.title: Reality Game
activities.body: Don’t know what to do between sessions? Want to interact with booths but don’t know how to start the conversation? Don’t worry—open OPass and join the Reality Game! This year’s Reality Game combines the SITCON 2026 theme, “Jam the Chaos.” In the game, you will unlock levels, challenge memory sequence games, master the rhythm, solve puzzles, accumulate completions, and climb the leaderboard for a chance to win discount vouchers for the SITCON 2026 souvenir booth! 【How to Unlock New Levels?】 🤝 Interact with Sponsors: Talk with sponsors to unlock new levels, verify higher-difficulty challenges, and sprint up the leaderboard! 🎤 Join Activities & Explore the Venue: Explore the venue, check in at activity locations, and participate in exciting events. Each activity and new location can unlock a new level~ 🔍 Meet New Friends: Interact with other attendees; every new encounter can unlock a new level. The more levels you complete and the higher you rank, the greater your chances of winning. Pick up your phone and groove with the rhythm!

activities.title: Whiteboard War
activities.body:
In this era of intertwined viewpoints and boundless creativity, pick up a whiteboard marker, add your thoughts to this chaotic world, and begin a wonderful clash of ideas!

We sincerely invite you to the whiteboard area on the 3rd floor to join this “Whiteboard War” without smoke or gunpowder. Jam the code together, debate, and defend the sect you believe in (x). This will be a grand platform for you to showcase creativity and let viewpoints collide.

In a relaxed atmosphere, let us use whiteboards as weapons, unleash boundless creativity, challenge different viewpoints, and create more sparks! Come splash your ideas across the board and make this whiteboard world even more chaotic!

activities.title: Meet the Speakers
activities.body:
Just finished an inspiring session, but still have questions you did not get to ask? Want to talk with the speakers about the stories and experiences behind their work? Don’t take those thoughts home unanswered!

Meet the Speakers is a great opportunity to have face-to-face conversations with speakers. After their sessions, some speakers will move to Hall S and wait there to chat with attendees. Whether you want to ask deeper questions about the session, discuss technical details, or simply exchange experiences, you are welcome to join.

The activity will take place from 15:25 to 16:45 in Hall S. Each speaker is expected to stay for 20 to 30 minutes. No preparation is required. Find the speaker you are interested in and say hello!

activities.title: Lightning Talks
activities.body:
Do you have something exciting to share, but missed the submission deadline? Welcome to SITCON 2026 Lightning Talks! Lightning Talks provide eight slots for the fastest attendees to share their projects or ideas with everyone in five minutes.

On the day of the conference, from 10:10 AM to 3:30 PM, you are welcome to submit your proposal at the designated whiteboard area. The submission method is simple: once submissions open, choose an empty slot on the whiteboard and write down your talk title and name (for example, “How I Became the Wind by Windful”). There are only eight slots, and each person may submit only once, so act fast! After submitting, please scan the form next to the whiteboard to provide more information, such as your title, speaker name, and outline. We suggest preparing this content in advance to ensure a smooth process.

If you successfully become a speaker, please gather at the right side of R0 on the 3rd floor at 4:40 PM sharp and bring your presentation equipment. Members of the Agenda Team will be there to assist you. To maintain diversity and fun, we do not accept overly commercial submissions. If your talk runs over time or includes commercial content for more than ten seconds, the screen will be cut immediately.

We invite you to join this adrenaline-pumping activity and share your ideas with more people at SITCON!

general.name: Core Organizing Team
general.description: The core team that coordinates the entire conference, oversees collaboration across all teams, and defines the conference’s direction and policies. We are the navigators of the conference, ensuring that the event runs smoothly and achieves its goals.

administration.name: Administration Team
administration.description: Handles all administrative affairs, including documentation, meeting minutes, and internal communication. We are the lubricant of the team, ensuring that every process runs smoothly.

agenda.name: Agenda Team
agenda.description: Responsible for program planning and speaker invitations, the Agenda Team is the core producer of the conference’s content. We review a large number of technical submissions and select the topics best suited for the conference, ensuring that attendees can hear high-quality content.

operations.name: Operations Team
operations.description: The guardians of the venue on conference day, responsible for traffic flow planning, equipment setup, and emergency response. We are the unsung heroes behind the smooth running of the event, and we need strong adaptability to face all kinds of challenges.

design.name: Design Team
design.description: Responsible for the key visual identity and merchandise design, depicting SITCON through colors and lines. We pursue visual excellence, transforming abstract concepts into concrete forms and bringing everyone a polished visual experience.

finance.name: Finance Team
finance.description: Manages the conference’s finances, ensuring that every dollar of sponsorship and budget is used effectively. We need meticulousness and patience to handle complex reimbursement, accounting, and budgeting work, serving as the solid backing behind the event.

recording.name: Documentation Team
recording.description: Captures every highlight of the conference through photography and video. We are the guardians of the conference’s memories, preserving every touching moment.

production.name: Production Team
production.description: Responsible for the conference’s audiovisual equipment and livestreaming systems, ensuring that every session is presented smoothly to all participants. We bring technology and art together so that every talk can be delivered at its best.

editorial.name: Editorial Team
editorial.description: Responsible for producing and proofreading all written content, including website copy, promotional materials, and press releases. We convey the spirit of SITCON through words, ensuring that every message reaches its target audience accurately.

marketing.name: Marketing Team
marketing.description: Responsible for external promotion and community management, helping more people discover SITCON through various channels. We are the spokespersons of the conference, promoting the event to the world with creativity and passion.

referral.heroSubtitle: Invite friends to join, and you can win prizes too

steps.title: Get Your Referral Code
steps.description: Once you successfully register for SITCON 2026, the system will automatically generate a unique referral code for you. You can check it anytime on your personal registration page.

steps.title: Share with Friends
steps.description: Share your referral code with friends, classmates, or community partners who have not registered yet, in any way you like.

steps.title: Friend Completes Registration
steps.description: When they enter your referral code during registration and complete the process, it counts as one successful referral.

items: The same referral code can be used by multiple people
The system will automatically accumulate your successful referral counts

lottery.title: One Lottery Entry per Referral
lottery.description: For every new registrant you successfully refer, you get one entry into the prize draw. The more you refer, the higher your chances of winning! Winners will be announced before 3/15 and notified by email, and the list will also be published below.

topReferrers.title: Top Referrer
topReferrers.description: The top five referrers with the most successful referrals will receive exclusive honors and SITCON 2026 commemorative gifts, and their names will be announced and awarded publicly at the SITCON 2026 conference!

fairness.title: Fairness and Notices
fairness.description: To ensure fairness, the organizers will:
items: Exclude referral records generated by duplicate, abnormal, or false registrations
Reserve the right to cancel eligibility if behavior affecting fairness is found
Use system data as the basis for all referral and prize draw records
fairness.disclaimer: The organizers reserve the right of final interpretation and adjustment of the event content, prizes, and rules.

about.title: About SITCON
about.description: SITCON is a student-driven community dedicated to IT education and the promotion of open source. It is also a student-organized technology conference that gives students a stage to present and exchange technical knowledge.

community.title: East Asia’s Largest Student Community for 13 Years

items.alt: Yahoo News: SITCON 2024 Conference Concludes Successfully
Newtalk News: SITCON Student Computing Conference

data.title: East Asia’s Largest Student IT Conference
data.description: Inspired the founding of SITCON x HK in Hong Kong and the Student Open Source Conference in Chongqing, and provides support to student groups and IT communities worldwide. More than half of the participants each year are first-time attendees. Alumni now work across the IT industry in Taiwan, the United States, Japan, and the Netherlands, and many continue to return and support the community.

data.title: Meetups Across Taiwan
data.description: Continuously co-hosting meetups with local communities across Taiwan, providing channels for students to exchange knowledge. These events have also partnered with universities such as National Taiwan University and Tamkang University, bringing together outstanding talents from the IT field.

data.title: SITCON Hour of Code
data.description: Since 2014, in collaboration with the Ministry of Education, we have regularly held Hour of Code events across Taiwan, sparking curiosity about the IT field among hundreds of children and students aged 4 to 16.

data.title: SITCON Hackathon
data.description: We held HackGen in 2014 and 2015, and in 2024 upgraded the event into the SITCON Hackathon. We hope participants can learn ideation, planning, and implementation through the hackathon, helping society and businesses discover student teams with strong potential.

data.title: SITCON Summer Camp
data.description: Since 2014, SITCON has held a summer camp every July, helping students interested in IT get started and igniting their passion for open source. Both campers and volunteers are students. In addition to the main curriculum, there are open source talks, a community expo, and a World Café, giving students opportunities not only to learn, but also to exchange ideas and spark inspiration.
